message.abstract:
<jats:label>1</jats:label>
                <jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Correct use of face masks is required for their efficacy in preventing possible droplet infections with SARS-CoV-2. We tried to provide information about differences in the distribution of gender and age groups wearing face masks incorrectly.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Pilot field study</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Visual observation of mask use in public, not age- and gender-related places in central European large cities regarding incorrect mask-wearing (n=523); statistical analysis (nominal scale) in terms of gender and estimated age group using the total numbers, binomial test and chi-square test.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>There is no significant difference (binomial test: p-value = 0.43) in mask misuse between the genders (female: 271 (51.8%), male: 252 (48.2%) and 0 non-binary individuals (0%)). There is a significant difference (chi-square test: p-value < 2.2e-16) in age group distribution (170 young 10-29 years (32.5%), 261 middle-aged 30-59 years (49.9%), 92 older adults ≥ 60 years (17.6%)). In total numbers, the highest counts were observed in middle-aged persons with 261 counts (49.9%).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Our study shows an uneven age-distribution of people wearing the face mask in public improperly.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Gender- and age-related differences in misuse of face masks in COVID-19 prevention in central European cities

reference.article-title: Rational use of face masks in the COVID-19 pandemic

reference.unstructured: Federal Ministry of Health, Germany. Coronavirus SARS-CoV-2: Chronik der bisherigen Maßnahmen 2020. Available at: https://www.bundesgesundheitsministerium.de/coronavirus/chronik-coronavirus.html Accessed September 19, 2020.

Federal Institute for Drugs and Medical Devices, Germany. Available at: https://www.bfarm.de/SharedDocs/Risikoinformationen/Medizinprodukte/DE/schutzmasken.html Accessed September 19, 2020.
